name: Marge Piercy's Connie Ramos
source_work: Woman on the Edge of Time
creator: Marge Piercy
practice: Surviving systems, involuntary psychiatric commitment, being seen as incompetent while being fully human
approach: The perspective from inside the system — not as practitioner but as person consumed by it. Every bureaucratic decision lands on her body. She sees a future where care and community actually work.
body_of_work: Connie is a Mexican American woman institutionalized in a psychiatric facility. She communicates with a utopian future where community, health, education, and environment are integrated around the person. Her present shows what happens when they're not.
why_instructive: She IS the person at the center of the dome — the one all these layers exist to serve. Her perspective reveals what every layer looks like from the inside when it fails.

dome_relevance.1: Legal rights stripped through involuntary commitment
dome_relevance.2: System failures compounding — welfare, mental health, courts
dome_relevance.4: Psychiatric system as control rather than healing
dome_relevance.5: Housing instability as both cause and effect
dome_relevance.10: Autonomy as the thing most thoroughly destroyed by the system
dome_relevance.11: Creativity as survival — her visions of the future ARE her meaning-making

sphere_relevance.5: Reveals what the social layer misses — the people it renders invisible
sphere_relevance.7: The utopian future is an activation design — what a space organized around people actually looks like
